question: Why was the K-200 battery chemistry changed from LiPo to LiFePO4?
answer:
Engineering Change Request ECR-214. [1] Engineering Change Notice ECN-312. [2] K-200 Battery System Specification [3]

Related records are linked in the knowledge graph (40 relationship paths surfaced).

citations.title: Battery cell chemistry change: P-1062 LiPo → LiFePO4
citations.passage:
ECR-214 | Battery cell chemistry change: P-1062 LiPo → LiFePO4

Engineering Change Request ECR-214.

Raised by: Lena Okafor (Battery & Power Systems Engineer), 2023-10-12.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2023-10-19.

Summary: Change the battery cell assembly (P-1062) from LiPo (6S2P 22.2V 20Ah) to LiFePO4 chemistry.

Rationale: The K-200 experienced a LiPo thermal runaway event on 8 September 2023 during a hot-day endurance test (KES-208). This was the second thermal anomaly on P-1062 cells (the first was a cell swelling event in March 2023). LiPo cells present an unacceptable risk under IEC 62133-2 compliance requirements for BVLOS maritime operations, particularly given the salt-water submersion risk profile. The customer contract (ref KES-208) explicitly prohibits LiPo chemistry for BVLOS maritime operations.

LiFePO4 chemistry offers superior thermal stability (stable to 270°C vs LiPo thermal runaway at ~150°C) at a cost of approximately 12% lower energy density. The endurance reduction from 75 minutes projected to 66 minutes is within the 60-minute minimum requirement. Trade study presented at WIKI-052 (Battery Chemistry Design Review, 2023-10-05).

Impact:
- P-1062: New LiFePO4 cell assembly (CellCore Technologies, 6S2P 22.2V 20Ah LiFePO4)
- P-1075: BMS module charge/discharge profile must be updated for LiFePO4 voltage curves (3.2V nominal vs 3.7V LiPo)
- P-1064: Battery connector to remain AS150 — no change
- DOC-421: Battery System Specification to be updated to Rev C
- P-1079: Battery wiring harness — no change required
- Qualification testing: Full cell-level and pack-level qualification per IEC 62133-2 required before ECN issue

Related: KES-208 (thermal runaway bug), WIKI-052 (design review meeting), DOC-421 (battery spec update).

citations.title: Approved Change — ECR-214: Battery cell chemistry change LiPo to LiFePO4
citations.passage:
ECN-312 | Approved Change — ECR-214: Battery cell chemistry change LiPo to LiFePO4

Engineering Change Notice ECN-312. Implements ECR-214.
Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2023-10-19.

This ECN formally authorises the implementation of ECR-214 (battery chemistry change). The change baseline is effective from ECN-312 approval date.

Authorised changes:
1. P-1062 (Battery Cell Assembly) updated to Rev C: LiFePO4 chemistry, 6S2P 22.2V 20Ah, supplier CellCore Technologies. Nominal cell voltage 3.2V (vs 3.7V LiPo). LiPo Rev B stock to be quarantined.
2. P-1075 (BMS Module) updated to Rev C: charge/discharge profiles updated for LiFePO4 voltage curves (3.2V nominal, 3.65V max, 2.5V min per cell). Hardware SOC limits remain per ECR-209 (18% floor / 82% ceiling).
3. DOC-421 (Battery System Specification) updated to Rev C: reflects LiFePO4 chemistry, updated energy density, updated BMS profiles.

Qualification status: Cell-level qualification per IEC 62133-2 completed (Aisha Mbeki, test report DOC-421 Rev C appendix). Pack-level qualification passed 2023-12-14.

citations.title: K-200 Battery System Specification v2.0 (post LiFePO4 transition)
citations.passage:
DOC-421 | K-200 Battery System Specification v2.0 (post LiFePO4 transition)

# K-200 Battery System Specification

Document number: MD-DOC-421-C. Revision: C. Type: specification. Author: Lena Okafor. Created: 2023-04-01. Last modified: 2023-12-14. Status: Released.

Subsystem: Battery.

## Change History

- Rev A: Initial LiPo baseline (2023-04-01)
- Rev B: BMS SOC limits updated per ECR-209 (2023-07-18)
- Rev C: LiFePO4 chemistry transition per ECN-312 / ECR-214 (2023-12-14)

## §1 Scope

This specification defines the battery system for the Kestrel K-200, covering cell chemistry, BMS requirements, safety limits, and qualification requirements.

## §2 Battery Cell Assembly — P-1062 Rev C

Chemistry: LiFePO4 (lithium iron phosphate). Configuration: 6S2P. Nominal voltage: 22.2V (3.7V/cell nominal). Capacity: 20Ah. Supplier: CellCore Technologies. Energy density: approximately 140 Wh/kg.

The transition from LiPo to LiFePO4 was driven by the thermal runaway event KES-208 (September 2023) and IEC 62133-2 compliance requirements for BVLOS maritime operations. Design review decision: WIKI-052 (2023-10-05). Change authority: ECN-312.

## §3 BMS Module — P-1075 Rev C

The BMS module manages cell balancing, SOC estimation, and safety protection. SOC limits (per ECR-209 and confirmed in REV-623 review commentary):
- SOC floor: 18% (hardware lockout — prevents cell reversal under 3C discharge)
- SOC ceiling: 82% (hardware lockout — prevents lithium plating at low temperature)

These limits are implemented in hardware independent of firmware to prevent single-point software fault bypass. See DOC-431 (BMS Safety Specification) for detailed rationale.

## §4 Electrical Interface

Main connector: P-1064 (AS150 gold 150A). Battery wiring harness: P-1079. Power distribution: P-1076.

sources.title: Battery cell chemistry change: P-1062 LiPo → LiFePO4
sources.body:
ECR-214 | Battery cell chemistry change: P-1062 LiPo → LiFePO4

Engineering Change Request ECR-214.

Raised by: Lena Okafor (Battery & Power Systems Engineer), 2023-10-12.
Status: Approved. Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2023-10-19.

Summary: Change the battery cell assembly (P-1062) from LiPo (6S2P 22.2V 20Ah) to LiFePO4 chemistry.

Rationale: The K-200 experienced a LiPo thermal runaway event on 8 September 2023 during a hot-day endurance test (KES-208). This was the second thermal anomaly on P-1062 cells (the first was a cell swelling event in March 2023). LiPo cells present an unacceptable risk under IEC 62133-2 compliance requirements for BVLOS maritime operations, particularly given the salt-water submersion risk profile. The customer contract (ref KES-208) explicitly prohibits LiPo chemistry for BVLOS maritime operations.

LiFePO4 chemistry offers superior thermal stability (stable to 270°C vs LiPo thermal runaway at ~150°C) at a cost of approximately 12% lower energy density. The endurance reduction from 75 minutes projected to 66 minutes is within the 60-minute minimum requirement. Trade study presented at WIKI-052 (Battery Chemistry Design Review, 2023-10-05).

Impact:
- P-1062: New LiFePO4 cell assembly (CellCore Technologies, 6S2P 22.2V 20Ah LiFePO4)
- P-1075: BMS module charge/discharge profile must be updated for LiFePO4 voltage curves (3.2V nominal vs 3.7V LiPo)

- DOC-421: Battery System Specification to be updated to Rev C

Related: KES-208 (thermal runaway bug), WIKI-052 (design review meeting), DOC-421 (battery spec update).

Document number: MD-DOC-421-C. Revision: C. Type: specification. Author: Lena Okafor. Created: 2023-04-01. Last modified: 2023-12-14. Status: Released.

Subsystem: Battery.

## Change History

- Rev B: BMS SOC limits updated per ECR-209 (2023-07-18)
- Rev C: LiFePO4 chemistry transition per ECN-312 / ECR-214 (2023-12-14)

## §1 Scope

This specification defines the battery system for the Kestrel K-200, covering cell chemistry, BMS requirements, safety limits, and qualification requirements.

## §2 Battery Cell Assembly — P-1062 Rev C

Chemistry: LiFePO4 (lithium iron phosphate). Configuration: 6S2P. Nominal voltage: 22.2V (3.7V/cell nominal). Capacity: 20Ah. Supplier: CellCore Technologies. Energy density: approximately 140 Wh/kg.

The transition from LiPo to LiFePO4 was driven by the thermal runaway event KES-208 (September 2023) and IEC 62133-2 compliance requirements for BVLOS maritime operations. Design review decision: WIKI-052 (2023-10-05). Change authority: ECN-312.

## §3 BMS Module — P-1075 Rev C

sources.title: Battery Cell Assembly — LiFePO4
sources.body:
P-1062 | Battery Cell Assembly — LiFePO4

Battery Cell Assembly — LiFePO4. Subsystem: Battery. Material: LiFePO4, 6S2P 22.2V 20Ah. Mass: 1820g. Manufactured by CellCore Technologies. Revision C. Part class: custom.

sources.title: Battery Chemistry Design Review — 2023-10-05
sources.body:
WIKI-052 | Battery Chemistry Design Review — 2023-10-05

Felix Herrera confirmed that the ~12% reduction in energy density from switching to LiFePO4 would reduce endurance from a projected 75 minutes to approximately 66 minutes at cruise — within the 60-minute minimum requirement.

Aisha Mbeki asked about the re-qualification test burden. Lena Okafor confirmed ECR-214 will capture the full impact including new qualification testing per DOC-421 Rev C.

LTO was ruled out: the energy density is too low to meet the 60-minute endurance requirement without exceeding the 8kg MTOW.

## Decision

**AGREED:** Transition the K-200 battery cell assembly (P-1062) from LiPo to LiFePO4 chemistry.

- Decision maker: Dr. Rhea Voss (Chief Systems Engineer)
- Rationale: IEC 62133-2 compliance requirement, two observed thermal events on P-1062 (KES-208 and March 2023 lab anomaly), acceptable energy density penalty
- ECR to be raised: ECR-214 (Lena Okafor to raise by 2023-10-12)
- Updated specification: DOC-421 (Battery System Specification) to be updated to Rev C
- BMS impact: P-1075 charge/discharge profiles must be updated for LiFePO4 voltage curves

## Actions

| # | Action | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | Raise ECR-214 for battery chemistry change | Lena Okafor | 2023-10-12 |
| A2 | Update DOC-421 to Rev C covering LiFePO4 spec | Lena Okafor | 2023-11-15 |
| A3 | Update P-1075 BMS charge profiles for LiFePO4 | Rowan Ellery | 2023-11-30 |
| A4 | Plan cell-level qualification test campaign | Aisha Mbeki | 2023-11-01 |

sources.title: Configuration Baseline — K-200 System Architecture v1.0 (per DOC-401)
sources.body:
ECN-301 | Configuration Baseline — K-200 System Architecture v1.0 (per DOC-401)

Engineering Change Notice ECN-301. Programme configuration baseline.
Approved by: Dr. Rhea Voss, 2023-04-15.

This ECN formally establishes the K-200 System Architecture configuration baseline as defined in DOC-401 (System Architecture Document v1.0).

Key baseline parameters:
1. Autonomy level: SAE Level 3 conditional automation (per customer RFT-K200-002 requirement, recorded in WIKI-003 kick-off meeting and REV-631 review comments)
2. MTOW: 8kg
3. Endurance target: 60 minutes minimum at cruise
4. Battery chemistry baseline: LiPo (subject to thermal risk note in WIKI-003) — subsequently changed by ECN-312
5. Propulsion: quad-pusher, 4x motors (subsequently P-1031, changed by ECN-318 to P-1041)
6. Comms: 900MHz FHSS, 15km range (P-1125, subsequently upgraded by ECN-322 to P-1126 with encryption)
7. GNC: single-string FCU (P-1091), BVLOS open-water operations only
8. Redundancy: hardware fail-safe RTL if datalink lost >10s

This baseline is the reference against which all subsequent ECRs and ECNs are compared.

# K-200 Battery Subsystem Specification

Document number: MD-DOC-416-B. Revision: B. Type: specification. Author: Nina Schulz. Created: 2023-04-28. Last modified: 2023-08-04. Status: Released.

This document defines the requirements, interfaces, and verification approach for the Battery subsystem of the Kestrel K-200. Referenced parts: P-1031, P-1062, P-1079.
